source_pr.title: infoschema: fix test for table rows count field
source_pr.body:
## What have you changed? (mandatory)

There will be a background loop that updates the stats when the stats lease is not 0. So when we call a function that updates stats elsewhere, there will be a chance of panic because there may have a race for the only stats session context.

## What are the type of the changes (mandatory)?

- Improvement (non-breaking change which is an improvement to an existing feature)

## How has this PR been tested (mandatory)?

unit test

PTAL @coocood @zimulala @zz-jason @winoros

symptoms: There will be a background loop that updates the stats when the stats lease is not 0. So when we call a function that updates stats elsewhere, there will be a chance of panic because there may have a race for the only stats session context.
failure_signature: There will be a background loop that updates the stats when the stats lease is not 0. So when we call a function that updates stats elsewhere, there will be a chance of panic because there may have a race for the only stats session context.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it depended on background/shared components (stats updater and DDL event propagation). A non-zero stats lease can start background stats update loops that race with other stats operations, and the information_schema view can lag until the DDL event is handled. This PR stabilizes the test by disabling the background stats lease (`SetStatsLease(0)`) and explicitly consuming/handling the DDL event before asserting.
fix_pattern: Disable background workers via global knobs (e.g. stats lease=0) and wait/handle DDL events to avoid shared-state interference in tests.

evidence: evidence: There will be a background loop that updates the stats when the stats lease is not 0. So when we call a function that updates stats elsewhere, there will be a chance of panic because there may have a race for the only stats session context.
